volumeTitle: Trojan Women. Iphigenia among the Taurians. Ion
label: Iphigenia among the Taurians (Section 1202 - Section 1290)

sections.label: Section 1202
sections.translation: Your piety and forethought are correct .

sections.label: Section 1203
sections.translation: Do you know what to do for me now ?

sections.label: Section 1203b
sections.translation: It is for you to make it known .

sections.label: Section 1204
sections.translation: Put chains on the strangers .

sections.label: Section 1204b
sections.translation: Where could they escape you ?

sections.label: Section 1205
sections.translation: Hellas knows no faith .

sections.label: Section 1205b
sections.translation: ( to his servants .) Go to get chains , attendants .

sections.label: Section 1206
sections.translation: And let them bring the strangers here .

sections.label: Section 1206b
sections.translation: It will be so .

sections.label: Section 1207
sections.translation: And veil their faces with their robes .

sections.label: Section 1207b
sections.translation: Before the light of the sun .

sections.label: Section 1208
sections.translation: And send some of your servants with me .

sections.label: Section 1208b
sections.translation: These will attend you .

sections.label: Section 1209
sections.original: καὶ πόλει πέμψον τινʼ ὅστις σημανεῖ —
sections.translation: And send someone to announce to the city —

sections.label: Section 1209b
sections.translation: That what has happened ?

sections.label: Section 1210
sections.translation: That all remain indoors .

sections.label: Section 1210b
sections.translation: So that they do not come in contact with murder ?

sections.label: Section 1211
sections.translation: Yes , for such things are polluted .

sections.label: Section 1211b
sections.translation: ( to a servant .) Go and announce —

sections.label: Section 1212
sections.translation: That no one come near the sight . An attendant departs .

sections.label: Section 1212b
sections.translation: You are taking good care of the city

sections.label: Section 1213
sections.original: καὶ φίλων γʼ οὓς δεῖ μάλιστα .
sections.translation: And of the friends to whom I owe the most .

sections.label: Section 1213b
sections.translation: You mean this for me .

sections.label: Section 1214b
sections.original: ὡς εἰκότως σε πᾶσα θαυμάζει πόλις .
sections.translation: The whole city marvels at you , with reason .

sections.label: Section 1215
sections.original: σὺ δὲ μένων αὐτοῦ πρὸ ναῶν τῇ θεῷ —
sections.translation: You stay here before the shrine of the goddess .

sections.label: Section 1215b
sections.translation: What shall I do ?

sections.label: Section 1216
sections.translation: Purify the house with fire .

sections.label: Section 1216b
sections.translation: So that you may return to find it pure .

sections.label: Section 1217
sections.original: ἡνίκʼ ἂν δʼ ἔξω περῶσιν οἱ ξένοι —
sections.translation: When the strangers come outside —

sections.label: Section 1217b
sections.translation: What must I do ?

sections.label: Section 1218
sections.translation: Hold your robe over your eyes .

sections.label: Section 1218b
sections.translation: So that I do not receive the pollution .

sections.label: Section 1219
sections.translation: If I seem to delay too long —

sections.label: Section 1219b
sections.translation: What limit of the delay should I keep in mind ?

sections.label: Section 1220
sections.translation: Do not wonder at it .

sections.label: Section 1220b
sections.original: τὰ τῆς θεοῦ πρᾶσσʼ — ἐπεὶ σχολή — καλῶς .
sections.translation: Carry out the rites of the goddess well , since you have leisure .

sections.label: Section 1221
sections.original: εἰ γὰρ ὡς θέλω καθαρμὸς ὅδε πέσοι .
sections.translation: May this purification fall out as I wish !

sections.label: Section 1221b
sections.translation: I pray along with you .

sections.label: Section 1222
sections.original: τούσδʼ ἄρʼ ἐκβαίνοντας ἤδη δωμάτων ὁρῶ ξένους
sections.translation: I see the strangers coming out of the temple now , and the ornaments of the goddess and the new-born lambs , because I will wash blood-pollution away with blood , and the flash of torches and all the rest that

sections.label: Section 1225
sections.original: προυθέμην ἐγὼ ξένοισι καὶ θεᾷ καθάρσια .
sections.translation: I have set out as purification for the strangers and the goddess .

sections.label: Section 1226
sections.original: ἐκποδὼν δʼ αὐδῶ πολίταις τοῦδʼ ἔχειν μιάσματος ,
sections.translation: I proclaim to the citizens to keep away from this pollution , if any guard of the temple is purifying his hands for the gods , or if anyone is coming to form a marriage alliance , or is weighted down by childbirth — begone , stand away , so that this defilement does not fall on anyone .

sections.label: Section 1230
sections.original: ὦ Διὸς Λητοῦς τʼ ἄνασσα παρθένʼ , ἢν νίψω φόνον
sections.translation: ( Aside .) O lady , maiden daughter of of Leto and Zeus , if I cleanse the stain of murder from these men , and make the sacrifice where I ought to make it , you will dwell in a pure home , and we will be fortunate . I do not speak the rest , but I indicate it to those who know more , the gods and you , goddess . Exit Iphigenia .

sections.label: Section 1.1234
sections.translation: Lovely is the son of Leto ,

sections.label: Section 1.1235
sections.original: τόν ποτε Δηλιὰς ἐν καρποφόροις γυάλοις
sections.translation: whom she , the Delian , once bore in the fruitful valleys , golden-haired , skilled at the lyre ; and also the one who glories in her well-aimed arrows .

sections.label: Section 1.1240
sections.translation: For the mother , leaving the famous birth-place , brought him from the ridges of the sea to the heights of Parnassus , with its gushing waters , which celebrate the revels for Dionysus . Here the dark-faced serpent

sections.label: Section 1.1245
sections.translation: with brightly colored back , his scales of bronze in the leaf-shaded laurel , huge monster of the earth , guarded Earth ’ s prophetic shrine . You killed him , o Phoebus , while still a baby ,

sections.label: Section 1.1250
sections.translation: still leaping in the arms of your dear mother , and you entered the holy shrine , and sit on the golden tripod , on your truthful throne

sections.label: Section 1.1255
sections.translation: distributing prophecies from the gods to mortals , up from the sanctuary , neighbor of Castalia ’ s streams , as you dwell in the middle of the earth .

sections.label: Section 1.1259
sections.translation: But when he came and sent Themis ,

sections.label: Section 1.1260
sections.translation: the child of Earth , away from the holy oracle of Pytho , Earth gave birth to dream visions of the night ; and they told to the cities of men the present ,

sections.label: Section 1.1265
sections.translation: and what will happen in the future , through dark beds of sleep on the ground ; and so Earth took the office of prophecy away from Phoebus , in envy , because of her daughter . The lord made his swift way to Olympus

sections.label: Section 1.1270
sections.translation: and wound his baby hands around the throne of Zeus , to take the wrath of the earth goddess from the Pythian home . Zeus smiled , that the child so quickly came

sections.label: Section 1.1275
sections.translation: to ask for worship that pays in gold . He shook his locks of hair , to put an end to the night voices , and took away from mortals the truth that appears in darkness ,

sections.label: Section 1.1280
sections.translation: and gave the privilege back again to Loxias , and to mortals confidence in the songs of prophecy at the throne visited by many men .

sections.label: Section 1284
sections.translation: O you that guard the temple and stand by the altar ,

sections.label: Section 1285
sections.original: Θόας ἄναξ γῆς τῆσδε ποῦ κυρεῖ βεβώς ;
sections.translation: where has Thoas , the lord of this land , gone ? Open the well-fastened gates , and call forth from this shrine the ruler of the land .

sections.label: Section 1288
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔστιν , εἰ χρὴ μὴ κελευσθεῖσαν λέγειν ;
sections.translation: What is it , if I may speak when not commanded ?

sections.label: Section 1289
sections.translation: The two young men are gone away ,

sections.label: Section 1290
sections.translation: through the plots of Agamemnon ’ s daughter ; they are escaping from this land , with the holy image deep within a Hellene ship .
